articles.title: AISA Calls June 1 Protest at Education Ministry, Demands Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation
articles.description: The All India Students Association (AISA) has called a protest outside the Education Ministry on June 1, demanding accountability over NEET, CBSE and CUET-related issues.

articles.title: French Open 2026: Gold-sequinned Osaka survives three-setter, to face Sabalenka next
articles.description: Naomi Osaka's dazzling French Open campaign continues as her sparkling play propelled her into the fourth round for the first time. The four-time major champion secured a hard-fought victory against Iva Jovic, setting up a highly anticipated clash with world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka. Osaka expressed a newfound calmness, embracing the journey.

articles.title: Just When Iran Deal Seemed Done, Trump Sends It Back for Revisions: Here's Why
articles.description: Trump's main concern relates to Iran's enriched uranium stockpile. The current draft does not contain detailed concessions beyond that pledge.

articles.title: UP Police Constable admit card 2026 likely to be released tomorrow at upprpb.in: Check steps to download hall ticket
articles.description: UP Police Constable admit cards for 2026 are anticipated tomorrow, June 1, on upprpb.in. The offline OMR-based exam is scheduled for June 8-10 across Uttar Pradesh. Candidates can expect city intimation details beforehand. Ensure you carry the admit card and valid ID to the exam centre. Stay tuned to the official website for updates.

articles.title: India poised to benefit as global wealth turns to AI, infra, emerging markets: Report
articles.description: Global family offices are increasingly directing capital toward artificial intelligence, infrastructure, energy transition and emerging markets as they reposition portfolios for a more uncertain world. A new UBS report suggests these trends could create significant opportunities for India across technology, infrastructure, private markets and gold investments.

articles.title: How a Gurgaon couple spent ₹1.59 lakh in March and still invested ₹2.2 lakh
articles.description: Gagan Saini shared an Instagram video last week where he explained how he and his wife managed to jointly spend ₹1.59 lakh in the month of March.

articles.title: Russia vs Ukraine Over Drone Strike on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant
articles.description: A drone struck a building at the Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in southeastern Ukraine on Saturday, causing damage but leaving core equipment intact. Ukraine denied responsibility for the attack.

articles.title: Choose your FD scheme: Fixed deposits vs Post Office deposits -- which offers better returns in June 2026?
articles.description: Fixed deposits and post office savings schemes remain two of India's most popular low-risk investment options, offering predictable returns and capital protection. While bank FDs score on flexibility and liquidity, post office schemes often provide higher sovereign-backed returns, especially for long-term investors and senior citizens.

articles.title: UP BEd Exam 2026 Begins Today; Check Shift Timings And Admit Card Details
articles.description: The UP BEd Exam 2026 starts on May 31 with two shifts scheduled from 9 AM to 12 noon and 2 PM to 5 PM.

articles.title: Indo-Tibet trade to resume via Shipki La from June 1: Negi
articles.description: Speaking to mediapersons on Saturday, Negi termed the reopening of the Shipki La trade route in Kinnaur as a significant step in reviving traditional Indo-Tibetan trade links

articles.title: Demand for Mhada flats falls 50% in two years
articles.description: For the 2,640 Mhada flats on offer across the city this year, the housing authority received 75,366 applications accompanied by deposit money, and the average number of applications per flat stood at 28.54

articles.title: HC seeks Punjab’s response on vacancies in education tribunal
articles.description: HC has directed the Punjab government to inform it about the steps taken to fill the vacant and soon-to-be-vacant posts before the next hearing scheduled on July 8

articles.title: Delhi building collapse: 9 rescued in Mehrauli, 6 feared trapped
articles.description: A fire was reported at a factory in Delhi's Mahipalpur. In a separate incident, a five-storey building collapsed in Mehrauli, where nine people have been rescued. A joint operation is underway as six others are feared trapped.

articles.title: England vs India, 2nd T20I: Freya Kemp's all-round show levels series
articles.description: Freya Kemp's all-round brilliance (39* & 2/15) guided England to a 26-run victory over India in the second T20I, leveling the series 1-1. Despite a good start, India's middle-order collapsed in the chase of 169, falling 26 runs short.

articles.title: Malaysia’s LGBTQ groups tread more carefully amid recent enforcement, ministers’ rhetoric
articles.description: Recent moves by the Malaysian authorities point to a more restrictive climate for LGBTQ people in the country, analysts say, and this could be due to political competition as well as the visibility of some events.

articles.description: Given disruptions in supply chains all over the world, the conflict has shown that Iran’s role in the global economy is not merely centred on oil, says this writer.

articles.title: Invisible walls: Caste in early learning spaces
articles.description: An opinion piece examines how caste bias shapes children's experiences in Anganwadis and preschools, from separate utensils to social exclusion. It highlights child rights, equality, nutrition, dignity and inclusive learning.

articles.description: SGPGI Lucknow transplants organs from brain-dead accident victim, benefiting five patients: two kidney, one liver and two cornea recipients across UP.

articles.description: Lucknow hosted ‘Sada-E-Zia Sufiana Shaam’ honoring Sufi poet Syed Zia Alvi Khairabadi, featuring qawwali, talks, and Fakhr-E-Khairabad awards.

articles.title: Cruise ship linked to deadly hantavirus outbreak cleared to sail after disinfection
articles.description: A cruise ship, the Hondius, has been cleared to resume sailing after a deadly hantavirus outbreak. The vessel underwent thorough cleaning and disinfection in Rotterdam. The outbreak led to passenger deaths and a global health alert. Most passengers were repatriated, and the ship's crew was quarantined. The World Health Organization confirmed thirteen cases linked to the incident.

articles.title: Two accused of raping DU studentsurrender before Lucknow court
articles.description: Two accused in alleged gang rape of 19-year-old Delhi University student in Lucknow surrender in court; police seek remand, probe continues for third suspect.

articles.title: PSG Defend UCL Crown With Shootout Win Against Arsenal In Budapest
articles.description: Ousmane Dembele's second-half penalty cancelled out Kai Havertz's opener, before shootout misses from Eberechi Eze and Gabriel proved costly for the Gunners in the final hurdle.

articles.title: Danish-assisted 24x7 water supply pilot put on hold in Jaipur
articles.description: Jaipur PHED halts Danish-backed 24x7 water supply pilot in nine areas amid summer demand spike and limited Bisalpur water; may restart in monsoon.

articles.title: Revival of Sabari rail project raises hopes in Central Travancore
articles.description: The revival of the Sabari rail project brings renewed hope for landowners in Central Travancore after decades of delays.

articles.description: Costs range from Rs 3,500- Rs 35,000 as the tickets went live at 6 p.m.

articles.title: Nuclear Risk Rising! Beijing Unleashes Scathing Attack On US Military Expansion
articles.description: The world is facing its most fragile strategic moment since the Cold War. In a powerful address at the Shangri-La Dialogue, Chinese Major General Meng Xiangqing dropped a massive warning to the West, stating that the risk of global nuclear war is no longer remote. Blasting the U.S.-led AUKUS pact for fueling nuclear proliferation and hammering Japan for trying to rewrite its pacifist constitution, Meng warned that a complete lack of dialogue has left a dangerous vacuum. Even more terrifyingly, he cautioned that unchecked military AI could fast turn 'Terminator' scenarios into reality. Watch China’s fierce pushback.

articles.description: M88, also known as NGC 4501, is classified as an active galaxy.
